Definition.text: Hex is a Curse category applied by Hex Curse skills; Curses are a type of Debuff.

Purpose.text: Apply a curse effect through an Area of Effect-based skill.

Core Rules.text: Hexes are spells that apply the curse in an Area of Effect. The term Hex by itself refers specifically to Hex Curse skills.

Internal Rules.text: Hex curse defaults are replaced by skill-specific values when cast; default and overridden values are per-skill.

Calculation Order.text: 1) Resolve skill hit/apply event 2) create curse debuff instance 3) apply curse limit checks 4) apply/removal/replacement rules for curses on the same target.

Priority Rules.text: On the same target, applying a different curse while limit is exceeded expires the oldest curse regardless of curse type or strength.

Preconditions.text: Target is not Immune to Curses; if hexproof applies only Hexproof marker without curseing stats.

Postconditions.text: Target gains a Hex debuff instance from the casting source and participates in the global curse limit and replacement model.

Limitations.text: Hexes cannot be applied to targets immune to Curses; hexproof interaction is distinct from removal rules.

Caps.text: Curse limit constraints are enforced as part of the Curses system; at default one curse is active per creature.

Formula.text: Curse Effect uses the Curse Effect formula: (sum added) * (1 + increases - reductions) * product(more) * product(less).

cannot_affect: target with Hexproof and no bypass condition

Conflicts.text: Hexproof targets can be considered cursed in state but do not gain debuffing stats unless bypass exists.

Exceptions.text: Hexproof is a conditional interaction edge; Hexes still affect cursed-state visibility but not debuffing stat application.

Hidden Rules.text: Linked hexes can be applied in one application chain; this does not change the base Hex category semantics.

Developer Notes.text: Treat Hex as a Curse subtype, not a standalone damage type; keep Area of Effect application and curse-limit behavior explicit.

Historical Changes.text: No explicit Hex-specific historical timeline appears in the current Curse page snapshot; see patch history entries where available.

text.note: Hex category is documented as Curse subtype and remains functionally separate from Mark curses.

text: A Hex applies a cursed state through an Area of Effect.
Applying another curse can remove or expire the oldest curse on the same creature under curse limit rules.
Hex is not equivalent to Mark.
Hex interaction with Hexproof is different from complete curse immunity.
